name: Vaccinia_MVA_deletion_regions
description: Modified vaccinia Ankara (MVA, reference U94848 / NC_006998 derivative) carries six large genomic deletions (Del-I through Del-VI) accumulated during >570 passages on chicken embryo fibroblasts, removing host-range and immune-evasion genes and rendering MVA replication-defective in mammalian cells. The deletions span ~31 kb total, eliminate ~31 ORFs, and are located in the variable terminal regions (Del-I, II, III in the left ITR-proximal region; Del-IV, V, VI in the right). MVA is the principal smallpox-vaccine alternative and a widely-used viral vector platform. Mapping clinical/laboratory MVA samples against the WR reference produces characteristic gap signatures at these six positions — discriminating MVA from wild-type vaccinia is essentially a deletion-pattern call.
expected_copy_count: 6 deletion intervals in MVA relative to WR reference
